prompt: My DSPy Predict module keeps throwing AdapterParseError. The model responds with the right content but does not use the [[ ## field ## ]] format. How do I fix this?
expected_output: Debugging steps: inspect_history to see what was sent/received, note that JSON fallback may already be handling it silently, and if not, switch to JSONAdapter for that module.
assertions: Suggests dspy.inspect_history() to see the actual prompt and response
Explains that ChatAdapter falls back to JSONAdapter automatically by default
Shows how to switch to JSONAdapter as a fix if fallback also fails
Does not suggest instantiating ChatAdapter unnecessarily when it is already the default

prompt: I want to use native function calling with DSPy instead of the text-based field delimiters. How do I enable it?
expected_output: Code showing ChatAdapter with use_native_function_calling=True, with a warning that not all providers support it.
assertions: Warns that not all providers support native function calling
Mentions native_response_types for limiting which types use native features
Uses provider-agnostic LM configuration with alternative comment

prompt: What is the difference between ChatAdapter and JSONAdapter in DSPy? When should I use each one?
expected_output: Comparison explaining ChatAdapter uses field delimiters while JSONAdapter uses JSON output format, with guidance on when to switch.
assertions: Explains JSONAdapter requests JSON output and uses json_repair for parsing
Notes JSONAdapter has use_native_function_calling=True by default while ChatAdapter has False
Recommends JSONAdapter for complex Pydantic types or when parse failures are costly
Notes ChatAdapter already falls back to JSONAdapter on failure
Mentions TwoStepAdapter as the option for reasoning models
